entry.title: Integration of Item Level Withholding Taxes of Ariba Invoice with SAP S4 HANA
entry.content:
Integration of Item Level Withholding Taxes of Ariba Invoice with SAP S4 HANA

When working on integration of Ariba B&I with SAP S4 HANA, in many of the cases customer and consultants face challenges when integrating withholding taxes with SAP. Ariba supports withholding taxes at header as well as at item level in Invoices, whereas SAP ERP design is such that it supports withholding taxes at Invoice Header only.

It is interesting to check why customers insists on having withholding taxes at item level in Ariba, there are many reasons to it, but main reason according to me is, if WHT is at line level, customers can leverage upon TaxCodeLookup.csv to automatically determine the applicable withholding taxes. Also, another reason is, it enables adding the withholding tax only to the specific goods or services that are subjected to withholding taxes there by avoiding any need to calculate base and tax amount manually unlike header level.

CIG supports integration of withholding taxes out of the box if added at header level. Withholding taxes added at line-item level requires custom mapping in CIG and enhancement in SAP ERP to accommodate the additional WHT data sent by CIG. In this blog we'll discuss the custom approach of integrating line level withholding taxes from Ariba to header level at SAP.

 

Overview of the solution:

Solution involves custom mapping in CIG to transform item level withholding tax information received from Ariba to header level withholding tax information to be sent to SAP ERP.

First and foremost, step in performing any custom mapping is to understand and analyze, pre-processing and processed payloads of different use cases. Once payloads are analyzed identify source and target conditions to ensure the mapping works for all the possible or required scenarios. After finalizing the design perform the custom mapping in CIG and test the mapping, after successful testing deploy it for all the required system IDs, this completes the mapping part from CIG.

Next step is to Implement an enhancement in SAP ERP to update Invoice Amount, WHT Data in case of repeating combinations and to delete blank lines.

With these steps we can achieve seamless integration of systems with transformation of line level withholding taxes from source structure to header level in target structure.

 

Detailed Solution:

Step 1: Creating Mapping in CIG.

Withholding Tax Data will be available in path mentioned below (Source Structure)

PaymentExportRequest -> Payment_PaymentTaxExport_Item -> item -> InvoiceReconciliation -> TaxDistributionVector -> item

Withholding Tax Data must be mapped to the path mentioned below (Target Structure)

ARBCIG_BAPI_INVOICE_CREATE -> WITHTAXDATA -> item.

Before mapping any fields, create a duplicate node for item available in WITHTAXDATA.

Creating Duplicate Node for Item

 

 

 

Once created it should be visible as shown below. Perform all the mapping in item #2.

Duplicate Item Node in structure

 First map the withholding tax type.

TaxType is available in PaymentExportRequest -> Payment_PaymentTaxExport_Item -> item -> InvoiceReconciliation -> TaxDistributionVector -> item -> TaxType -> UniqueName field. Draw a line connecting UniqueName with WI_TAX_TYPE as shown below.

Tax Type Mapping

Now add a position variable on repeating nodes and define source conditions to ensure the mapping works only if data is relevant for WHT. I have used debit credit indicator and negative tax amount as source conditions. Incase if additional taxes with negative tax amount other than WHT exists, source condition needs to be updated accordingly.

Conditions on Tax Type Mapping

 Similarly perform mapping for Tax Code, Base Amount and Tax Amount.

Mapping Tax Code, Base Amount and Tax Amount

Make sure to add format number function to restrict the length of decimal places to 4 when mapping taxable amount and tax amount, reason being, Ariba supports up to 5 decimal places in these fields, whereas SAP ERP supports only 4. Hence format number function is required in mapping to avoid serialization error in SAP.

Ariba Field Specification

SAP Fields Specification

Add a constant to SPLIT_KEY  = “000001”. Specifying the source condition same as other mappings to ensure, constant value is passed only if applicable.

For repeating node, “For Each” functionality can be used.

Save the mapping and test it.

Test Result Screen for Mapping

 This completes the mapping from CIG Side.

 

Step 2: Implementing an enhancement in SAP.

It is important to know why an enhancement is required in SAP!

Whenever withholding taxes are added in Ariba, Ariba subtracts withholding tax amount from gross amount of the invoice, whereas in SAP Total invoice amount should consider withholding tax amount as well, which will later be posted to separate GL mapped to WHT. When withholding taxes are added at header level in Ariba, standard mapping adds Invoice Amount and WHT Amount and passes the gross amount to SAP.

Standard Mapping Details for Gross Amount

In case, where WHT are added at line level, IsHeaderLevelLine won’t be equal to “TRUE” hence WHT is not added to Gross Amount. This requires XSLT mapping, to avoid XSLT mapping, alternative option is to perform enhancement in SAP.

Also, the blank lines are created in structure WITHTAX data where our preconditions are not met for mapping, hence enhancement is required to delete the lines from “BAPIWITHTAXDATA where WI_TAX_CODE is initial”.

It is possible that Invoice with multiple lines may have same tax type and code on more than one line, so enhancement is required to sum up Taxable Amount and Tax Amount to keep only 1 line for each tax code and tax type, deleting duplicate lines from invoice to any avoid error related to duplicate entries for same tax type.

The enhancement can be added in BAdI “ARBCIG_INVCREATE” in method “IF_ARBCIG_EX_INVOICE_CREATE~INVOICE_CREATE”, only if PO Based Invoices are to be considered. For FI Invoices, implicit enhancement can be added in FM "ARBCIG_BAPI_INVOICE_CREATE". which will work for PO and Non-PO Invoices both

This completes the enhancement in SAP side which will allow us to post PO and Non-PO Invoices in SAP with Integration of Withholding Taxes from Item Level (Ariba) to Header Level (SAP).

Example:

Ariba Non-PO Invoice

Snapshot of Ariba Invoice with WHT at line level

 Non-PO Invoice posted in SAP through CIG.

Snapshot from SAP showing Accounting Entries generated

 

Based on the discussion from the previous sections of the blog, we can conclude below points,

  1. SAP Ariba supports Withholding Taxes at Header Level as well as at Item Level.
  2. CIG Supports Integration of Header Level Withholding Taxes out of the box, where as Item Level Withholding Taxes through Custom Mapping.
  3. Custom Mapping can be added as per the requirement, specifying vide range of source and target conditions as required/ applicable.
  4. Enhancement in SAP is must to update the data to make it free from errors, and to ensure successful posting of Invoice in SAP.

entry.title: Creating a Digital Thread: Streamlining Price Compliance with SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Price compliance is a key priority for procurement organizations. SAP Ariba Procurement offers features to ensure organizations make purchases at negotiated prices and terms. Integration between sourcing, contracts, purchase orders, and invoices is essential for adhering to negotiated prices throughout the procurement process. This blog explores how SAP Ariba products start that digital thread that creates a strong linkage for price compliance.

In our previous blog, we discussed sourcing principles for creating a fair and equitable marketplace. We recommend creating events with line items to begin price compliance. While it may seem easier to send suppliers RFPs via Excel or Outlook, line items in Sourcing offer more than you might realize.

Integration of line item details can occur at various stages post-award, directly from Sourcing to your ERP, from your Contract to ERP, from Contract to Ariba Catalog, or from Contract to Invoice. Integration depends on the integrations you enable and the buy channel that supports the goods or services requested.

SAP Ariba products integrate as a suite from S2P, and have seamless integrates to support buying from within your SAP ERP systems and the SAP Business Network, offering several benefits:

  • Consistency and accuracy across procurement processes.
  • Reduced manual effort and minimized errors.
  • Efficient tracking and monitoring of price compliance metrics.
  • Enhanced visibility into procurement spend and contract utilization.

To optimize price compliance, organizations should:

  • Analyze historic spend data for opportunities in buy channel efficacy.
  • Understand robust data integration between SAP Ariba products and SAP ERP systems.
  • Conduct regular audits and reconciliation of line items across procurement documents.
  • Provide training and guidance for procurement teams on maintaining price compliance for various buy channels.

Now that you have your Line Items ready to Award in Guided Sourcing, let’s review the various options for compliance:

Line Items in Sourcing: In SAP Ariba Guided Sourcing, line items capture detailed information about products/services, quantities, and negotiated prices. Integration with SAP ERP updates Purchase Info Records, automatically populating purchase orders and reducing errors.  Next, let’s review how Ariba Contract Compliance can ensure compliance.

Transferring Price Compliance From Contracts: SAP Ariba Contracts integrates with SAP ERP to transfer price information from sourcing events to contract documents. Contract Line Items Document (CLID) aligns with sourcing line items, ensuring consistent pricing and terms, streamlining contract creation in SAP ERP. 

If you would like to purchase from SAP Ariba, the Contract Terms document can create a Catalog Item for users to create POs from. Let’s review some options for Contract Compliance through Ariba Catalogs or Invoices:

Contract Terms: Integration with Purchase Orders: After a supplier is chosen in SAP Ariba Guided Sourcing, line item details go to the Contract Workspace for operationalization. Users can create an operational Contract Terms document to ensure price compliance in purchase orders.  This can create a catalog item in Ariba to create a PO that complies with the terms of the contract! 

For other types of contracts, it may make more sense to simply allow suppliers to submit an invoice against the contract terms.  Let’s talk about Invoice against contract next.

Contract Terms: Ensuring Compliance in Invoices: Contracts can be created to ensure compliance with recurring spend without a PO.  Invoices reconcile line items against the Contract Terms, detecting discrepancies and enforcing adherence to negotiated prices.  This works well with services that need to happen and the time for creation and approval of a PO is not possible.  These Contract Terms can allow the supplier to invoice against the contract without a PO.

SAP Ariba Suite Integration with or without ERP integration can transfer agreed upon pricing and terms from Sourcing to Contracts to Buying to Invoicing and ensure procurement objectives are achieved.  SAP Ariba products offer the tools and features needed to build a solid base for price compliance, promoting accuracy, efficiency, and transparency across the procurement process.

For further discussion on enhancing your price compliance using these integration methods, please reach out to your CSP, who will connect you with the appropriate SAP resource.

entry.title: SAP Procurement (Ariba and Business Network solutions) - Customer Onboarding Webinar Series
entry.content:
The Customer Onboarding Webinar series for Ariba and Business Network for Procurement is split into a series of three webinars:

  • Session one covers how to get started with your new solution
  • Session two covers an overview of your new solution and the services available
  • Session three covers how to engage with Support and prepares you and your team for go-live

We encourage you and your colleagues to attend the series of webinars or watch a past recording via the links below:

Webinar - Live

SAP Intelligent Spend and Business Network

Webinar – On demand

SAP Ariba

Additionally, please visit the SAP Ariba Procurement  for supplementary documentation on your path to success. For SAP Ariba and Business Network for Supply Chain and BN for Commerce Automation , please contact SAP Support Portal Home

entry.title: Shape the Future of SAP Solutions: Join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative 2024 Cycle 2
entry.content:
Exciting news for our valued SAP customers and partners! The second cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative in 2024 has officially kicked off today, bringing with it 50 new projects to fuel innovation and collaboration.

We understand the importance of staying ahead of the curve in today's fast-paced business landscape. That's why we're excited to offer you the opportunity to gain early insights into SAP's product developments. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, you'll have the chance to work directly with our developers and product managers, shaping the future direction of our products.

But time is limited! The projects will be open for customer registration until July 12, 2024. Don't miss out on this opportunity to make your voice heard and influence the next generation of SAP's products and solutions.

To help you navigate through the projects, we've compiled a comprehensive list for your convenience. Check out the whole list here or follow the following links to explore the possibilities:

SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • AI assistant for General Ledger Accountant
  • AI-based warehouse order streaming, task optimization and resource planning
  • Availability Check in Two-Tier ERP Subsidiary Business
  • Basic End-to-end make-to-order / assemble-to-order in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • GRC 2026 for SAP GRC Solutions Private Cloud Edition / On-Premise
  • Intelligent assistant capabilities for Project Systems in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Lockout Tagout / Isolation Management in Permit to Work
  • Risk and Assurance Reporting supported by AI
  • SAP Project and Resource Management - Scheduling of projects
  • Taulia receivables financing with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • Adaptive User Interface powered by GenAI
  • AI Agents - A Low-Code / No-Code Process Automation Innovation
  • Enterprise automation with SAP: Integrate - Identify - Automate
  • Governance Blueprints & Exchange - Govern and monitor developments with SAP Build
  • Improving the Admin's and Solution Architect's Experience on SAP BTP
  • SAP Business Technology Platform - Data Protection and Privacy services
  • SAP Gateway Fiori Notifications
  • SAP S/4HANA Extensibility using SAP Build, SAP Build Code and ABAP
  • SAPUI5 2.x

AI

  • Automotive Industry: Explore AI Use Cases in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Insights into SAP BTP AI Innovations

Lifecycle Management

  • SAP Focused Run
  • Simplified Implementation Experience in SAP Cloud ALM

Data & Analytics

  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)
  • User Validation for new SAP Datasphere homepage

User Assistance

  • Moving features into the Manager component of SAP Enable Now
  • Support of generative AI in SAP Enable Now

SAP Globalization

  • Localization as a Self-Service for SAP S/4HANA Public Edition
  • Note Analyzer Improvements

SAP Concur

  • Concur Expense - "Expense Reimagined" Concept
  • Concur Travel and Concur Request - Pre-booking approval
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Company Notes on the SAP Concur Homepage
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Concur Home Page Redesign
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Extensibility and External Integration
  • SAP Concur Solutions – Ideas for Future of Travel and Expense Card Spend Controls
  • SAP Concur Solutions - Joule (Digital Assistant)
  • SAP Concur Solutions – Support Knowledge Base Articles- Customer Experience and Feedback
  • SAP Concur Solutions – The Future of Data Delivery Service
  • SAP Integration with SAP Concur Solutions - Accruals Handling
  • SAP Integration with SAP Concur Solutions - Payroll integration

SAP Ariba

  • Embedded Virtual Card Payments for SAP Ariba Procurement and SAP Business Network using Taulia
  • SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management - Potential UI enhancements
  • Shape with us the future of mobile procurement

SAP Learning

  • Protean - Revolutionize Learning with GenAI and Immersive Technologies

Customer Experience

  • Field Service Management - Horizon adoption

SAP Integration Experience Feedback goes SAP Customer Engagement Initiative

  • Assisted Assessment of End-to-End implementation based on SAP Ariba Guided Buying
  • Hands-on System Experience of the Integrated Business Process Order to Cash within Lead to Cash 
  • Hands-on-System Experience of the Intelligent Enterprise Scenario - External Workforce

Others

  • Industrial Metaverse
  • SAP Cloud ALM or Implementation: Customer Engagement Council

We believe that collaboration is key to unlocking innovation. By joining forces with our customers and partners, we can collectively shape the future of SAP’s products and change the way businesses operate.

So, mark your calendars, gather your team, and let's embark on this journey together. Together, we can drive progress, transform industries, and create a brighter future.

Don't delay – register today on influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 in 2024!

Warm regards,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Join Customer Engagement Intiative for Embedded Virtual Card Payments for SAP Ariba Procurement
entry.content:
Virtual credit card payments for Vendors/Suppliers using SAP Ariba and SAP Taulia can help stretch your Working Capital by providing "FREE" credit for upto 60 days - the time between card charge and paying the Bank after card statement, and earn cash rebates reducing the cost of the purchase. Virtual card payments are better alternative to Procurement Cards - since it is single use, specific to a Supplier, specific to PO or Invoice, has a maximum amount specified to be charged and offers other payment controls to make transaction secure which can be easily reconciled with Accounts Payable (AP) transaction records.

Help us build the right product for making Virtual Credit Card payments from SAP Ariba, by joining (registering) this Customer Engagement Initiative: Embedded Virtual Card Payments for SAP Ariba Procurement and SAP Business Network using Taulia (https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3707)

Virtual cards are becoming increasingly essential in procurement applications like SAP Ariba. Virtual cards are revolutionizing B2B payments by offering a secure, efficient, and transparent alternative to traditional methods like checks or physical cards. Virtual cards are digitally generated, single-use or limited-use credit card numbers used for specific B2B transactions. Like physical cards, they have 16-digit numbers, expiration dates, and CVVs, but exist only online. Businesses can generate virtual cards through online platforms provided by financial institutions or fintech companies. They can set predefined spending limits, vendor restrictions, tie to particular purchase order(s) or invoice(s) or purchasing transaction(s), set activation and expiration dates for each card, ensuring tight control over expenses. Once created, the card details can be used for online payments or through mobile wallets for in-person transactions. Integrating virtual card functionality into procurement applications like SAP Ariba offers a multitude of benefits for both buyers and suppliers, streamlining the procurement process, enhancing security, and boosting overall efficiency.

Depending on the source, there are varying statistics regarding B2B virtual card usage. RFI / Visa study says the global value of virtual card transactions is expected to increase from $1.9 trillion in 2021 to an astronomical $6.8 trillion by 2026 and the global B2B virtual card market is expected to reach a staggering USD 1,291.05 billion (or USD $1.3 Trillion) by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 21.15% (Straits Research).

Benefits for B2B buyers:

  • Enhanced security: Eliminates risks associated with physical cards, like loss, theft, or data breaches. Each transaction generates a unique number, minimizing fraud exposure.

  • Improved payment control: Predefined spending limits and vendor restrictions ensure compliance with company policies and prevent unauthorized purchases.

  • Streamlined reconciliation: Real-time transaction data simplifies expense tracking and reconciliation, saving time and resources.

  • Faster payments: Virtual cards facilitate instant payments, improving supplier relationships and potentially earning early payment discounts.

  • Increased transparency: Detailed transaction data provides valuable insights into spending patterns, helping optimize budgets and negotiate better deals with vendors.

Benefits for B2B sellers:

  • Guaranteed payment: Reduces the risk of bounced checks or delayed payments.

  • Faster transaction processing: Streamlined payment process leads to quicker funds receipt.

  • Simplified reconciliation: Virtual cards provide consistent data format for easier reconciliation.

  • Improved cash flow: Faster payments and reduced manual processing contribute to smoother cash flow management.

Don't delay – register today on Embedded Virtual Card Payments for SAP Ariba Procurement and SAP Business Network using Taulia / https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3707 and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 in 2024!

entry.title: Getting Started with SAP Ariba Shopping App in 7 steps
entry.content:
Administration guide for SAP Ariba Shopping with interactive onboarding diagram

Hello Ariba community,

I would like to share with you the latest product update: The new SAP Ariba Shopping administration guide is now online!

The administration guide provides you with an interactive onboarding diagram with all required steps and roles for a successful rollout. This blog post helps you with additional screenshots and useful hints in completing the 7 administrative tasks in the administration guide. It was never easier to get started with SAP Ariba Shopping!

Before you start, check the following prerequisites:

You have a valid subscription for one of the following Ariba purchases:

  • SAP Ariba Buying or
  • SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing

You have administration rights for the following systems:

  • SAP BTP Global Account of your Ariba purchase 
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services / Corporate IDP
  • SAP Ariba Developer Portal

You are aware that the optional integration from guided buying to S/4 is not supported:

The SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app doesn't support requisitions created and submitted in guided buying when guided buying is integrated with SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud (2NV and 3EN).

 

Step 1: Subscribe to SAP Shopping App with SAP Booster

A SAP Booster is a set of guided interactive steps that enable you to select, configure, and consume services on BTP. Search for the SAP Ariba Shopping Booster to subscribe to the BTP application.

You have to start the Booster at the BTP global account of your SAP Ariba purchase to subscribe to SAP Ariba Shopping. You can access BTP global accounts with the SAP BTP cockpit: https://emea.cockpit.btp.cloud.sap

Your company can have multiple BTP global accounts. A global account represents the sales contract and the subscriptions of the purchased solutions.  If you don't find the BTP global account of your SAP Ariba purchase, ask your SAP contact person (CSP - Customer Success Partner) or create a support ticket. For more information, see Contacting Support.

Read this section in the administration guide. Click on start.

SAP BTP Global Account, tab Boosters

Check Prerequisites 

The Booster starts with checking the prerequisites:

SAP Booster: Check Prerequisites

If the authorization check fails, you probably have no global account administrator rights. If the entitlements check fails, the global account does not belong to the Ariba purchase. If you do not find the global account for the Arba purchase, contact your SAP contact or create a support ticket.

Click on Next when the checks are successful.  

Select Scenario

SAP Booster: Select Scenario

You can create or select from existing subaccounts. If you select an existing subaccount you may skip to configure the SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) tenant because this step is probably already completed. For this blog post, I select the option to create a new subaccount. Click on Next.

Configure subaccount

Enter now the subaccount details. To minimize network latency, BTP data center and Ariba data center should be in the same region. Click on Next.

SAP Booster: Configure Subaccount

Select Identity provider for platform and application and add users

In this step you select the identity provider for the platform and the identity provider for the application. In a productive scenario you would select the identify provider which is used to identify Ariba end users. In a testing scenario you can select the identify provider where you are authorized to create users. 

Furthermore, you can add other colleagues (you are already selected per default) to subaccount administrator role and developer role.  The developer role is needed to access the SAP Mobile Service cockpit.

SAP Booster: Add Users

Review before automation starts

After reviewing the input, the Booster makes the following manual tasks for you. Click on Finish.

SAP Booster: Review

Step 2: Configure Identify Authentication Service

In this step you establish trust between SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) of the application and the subaccount with the SAML 2.0 protocol. Read this section in the admin guide to complete on this common task.

To create test users read this sap help section where, you assign the user to Purchasing User and Mobile Shopping user groups at SAP Ariba Buying (P2P). In the IAS you can use the Login attribute to identify the Ariba end user. Then you maintain the SAP Ariba User Id in the login attributes of the test user.

SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) : Maintain Login Name as identifier (left), maintain Login Name (right)

Step 3: Create Application Definitions in SAP Ariba Developer Portal

Sign in to the SAP Ariba APIs developer portal:

SAP Ariba Developer Portal - Login screen

Use the recommended namings from this section in the administration guide and create four application definitions.

SAP Ariba Developer Portal: Example Application Definition

Note down the generated application keys. You do not have to request API access. You also do not have to link the applications to a public Ariba API. The SAP support team will assign the applications to private APIs when the service request is processed. The service request you will create in the next step 4 will process the next three steps in the process (see screenshot, "What's next"-Section)  

SAP Ariba Developer Portal: Approval workflow for application

Step 4: Create Service request to enable SAP Ariba Shopping APIs

Read this section in the admin guide. Download the the SAML XML file of the BTP destination to add the file as an attachment to the service request:

SAP BTP subaccount: Destinations

Create the service request (support component: BNS-ARI-SAS-API) in SAP for Me with the following content:

Dear SAP Support,

The information to enable the SAP Ariba APIs is as follows.

  • Site Name: The site name (realm) where you want to connect the app with

  • Data Center: The data center from the Ariba Developer Portal where you have created the four applications 

  • ANID: [Example: AN01037605217-T] - The technical id of the Ariba realm where you want to connect the app with

  • Application Keys:

    • SAS Procurement Requisition: [application key]

    • SAS Procurement Chooser: [application key]

    • SAS User Schema Information: [application key]

    • SAS Catalog Search: [application key]

Attached is the SAML XML metadata file from the SAP BTP subaccount.

The processing of the service request takes usually one or two working days. Once completed, you see in the developer portal that the applications are approved for the Ariba Shopping APIs. Request now the oAuth client secrets for the four applications from your Ariba administrator. Note down also the Oauth Client id for the four applications and the real name ("cirrusatlanticsuite" in my example) for later use in step 6.

SAP Ariba Developer Portal: application status after processed service request

Step 5: Connect SAP Mobile Services to SAP Ariba Shopping

Read this section in the administration guide. Select the subaccount in the SAP BTP cockpit. Click on SAP Ariba Shopping application:

SAP BTP cockpit: subaccount

The URL to access the SAP Mobile Service Cockpit is shown in the browser:

SAP BTP cockpit: output of SAP Ariba Shopping application

Copy and bookmark the SAP Mobile Service URL and note down the mobile Destination URL for the next step. Open now the SAP Mobile Service URL to access the SAP Mobile Service cockpit, Kernel Service.

The Kernel Service is used for an application like SAP Ariba Shopping that is managed in a single-app cockpit (Software as a Service, or SaaS). The license type is read-only and cannot be selected or edited in the SAP BTP cockpit. 

Go to tab "info", select feature "Mobile Connectivity":

SAP Mobile Service Cockpit: Start section with assigned features

You see three mobile destinations:

SAP Mobile Service Cockpit: Destinations to SAP Ariba

To configure the three destinations you need first obtain from SAP Ariba Developer Portal the following:

  • The value of the host of the Production & Test URL. Example: https://openapi.ariba.com. 
  • The value of the Token Service URL: Example: https://api.ariba.com/v2/oauth/token

SAP Ariba Developer Portal: Section Discover

Update all three destinations with the right values from the admin guide. Every destinations requires slightly different information. I selected the images destination as an example:

Basic Info

SAP Mobile Service cockpit: Enter Basis Info

Custom Headers

In the second step you enter the application key of the Ariba application "SAS Catalog Search":

SAP Mobile Service Cockpit: Custom Headers

Annotations

Click on Next.

Destination Configuration

Read the step in admin guide to update the fields with the right values.

SAP Mobile Service Cockpit: Destination configuration

Certificate Configuration

Click on Finish. When you have updated all three destinations you should see a similar result:

SAP Mobile Service cockpit: All destinations updated

Click on the row of the requisition-attachment to access the security section. Download SAML metadata file and set expiry date (maximum is 10 years). Update the created Service Report with the SAML metadata file to enable the API for the attachment functionality.

SAP Mobile Service Cockpit: Access security section of destination

Step 6: Configure SAP BTP Destination To SAP Ariba

Read this section in the admin guide. Go to the Destination section of the subaccount and update the four Destinations: 

BTP Cockpit: Update 4 destinations

Here is an example how I updated the first destination. The warning is not applicable for this step.

SAP cockpit: Example to update first destination

Check the connection for all four destinations. A green checkmark and a message that says the connection is established indicates that your configuration was successful. The returned response doesn't matter even if it is HTTP Status code 403 or 404.

 

Step 7: Onboard Users to SAP Ariba Shopping

Read this section in the admin guide. Go to SAP Mobile Service cockpit, navigate to API tab to view the QR code:

SAP Mobile Service Cockpit: API tab
 
  1. Download (iOS/Android) and open SAP Ariba Shopping app 
  2. Scan QR Code and check connection request
  3. Agree to end user license agreement (EULA)
  4. Start shopping!
SAP Ariba Shopping app on iOS

Further References:

  • Watch SAP Ariba Shopping app on Youtube.
  • Our roadmaps for iOS and Android.
  • Blog post from my colleague Jens Trummer about the first release

entry.title: Enhanced Continuous Quality Check for SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network Now Available
entry.content:
“Continuous quality checks” are individual services delivered by SAP Enterprise Support and are available with every cloud subscription. The remote-service reports help you reduce technical risks and identify areas for optimization potential, based on real-life data from your systems or solution.

The revamped Continuous Quality Check (CQC) for deployment readiness for SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network is now available. This CQC is designed to provide an SAP best practice review for key settings in your SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network instance.

View the CQC info sheet for full details.

Solutions Covered by This Service

  • SAP Ariba Sourcing
  • SAP Ariba Contracts
  • SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance
  • SAP Ariba Supplier Risk NEW
  • SAP Ariba Buying
  • SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing
  • SAP Ariba Catalog
  • SAP Ariba Invoice Management
  • SAP Business Network for Procurement
  • SAP Business Network Commerce Automation NEW
  • SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration NEW
  • SAP Integration Suite, managed gateway for spend management and SAP Business Network NEW

Key Benefits

  • Review current settings, use of features, and enablement of key functions.
  • Identify concerns, issues and potential risks prior to deployment/go-live.
  • Receive a detailed service report that highlights key areas for consideration and SAP best practice recommendations.
  • Optimize the possibility for a smooth start of production.

When to Use

  • Before the go-live of a new SAP Ariba implementation.
  • When additional solutions are added to the existing SAP Ariba suite.
  • As a "health check" for productive SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network systems.

How to Request

  • Remote Services can be requested via the new Get Support application in SAP for Me (SAP Note 1296527). When submitting your request, please select:
  1. Product: “Continuous Quality Check & Improvement Services”
  2. Product function: “Service request for a Cloud Solution”
  • If you need assistance in submitting your case, you may contact your local Customer Interaction Center (CIC).
  • SAP recommends service booking of six (6) weeks prior to go-live date, to allow for the delivery four (4) weeks before your go-live date and provide adequate time to review and implement any SAP recommendations (attempts will be made to accommodate requests made outside this timeframe, but are not guaranteed).
  • SAP will evaluate if this service is suitable for your business needs.
  • SAP recommends the service to be performed on the productive tenant, if not available it can be performed on the test tenant.

Achieve More with SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps

Find the right content to accelerate the value of your solution with the Spend Management and Business Network value map from SAP Enterprise Support. Included with every SAP cloud subscription, value maps offer structured, product-level Learning Rooms where you can navigate to curated content, be directed to self-service platforms, engage with fellow customers and SAP experts, and discover continuous quality checks to mitigate implementation risks or identify optimization potential.

Explore how SAP technology can transform your procurement operation or make your existing software work harder for you by visiting value maps today.

Access Value Maps in 3 Easy Steps:   

  1. One-time sign-up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required   
  2. Access value maps 
  3. Navigate to the SAP Enterprise Support value map for Spend Management and Business Network 

Once in the value map, we welcome you to collaborate in our forums, ask questions or “Request a Call” to speak with one of our experts for further help. Value maps are easy to navigate but an on-boarding call allows us to tailor the conversation to suit your needs and highlight specific content for you. 

Got a question about value maps, or our content?

📺Watch our short video.

Leverage the “ask questions” feature in the value map or email us:
sapesvaluemaps@sap.com

entry.title: Maximize Value with Value Analytics for SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Anyone looking out for some analytics on SAP Ariba solutions?

Starting with SAP Analytics Cloud Content Release Q2 2024 you will find a new package in your SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere semantic onboarding section by the name of Value Analytics for SAP Ariba Procurement (SAP Datasphere).

The content included serves as a broad foundation to analyze all steps across your sourcing and procurement processes done in the SAP Ariba solution suite. It comes with quite a number of data models in SAP Datasphere that will allow you to get most of your questions answered like

  • how is your company's spend behavior?
  • how can the sourcing / procurement processes be optimized?
  • how can you leverage savings potentials?

You can take this as a starter to get going. And as always you are free to enhance these data models bringing your own data and models for extension cases to get the big picture and reflect data of your company's end-2-end solution landscape.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud Stories

The package comes with three SAP Analytics Cloud stories to give you an idea of what can be built on top of the delivered SAP Datasphere data models:

1. SAP Ariba Source to Pay - Procurement Strategic Spend Overview

This story provides you with insights into the value your sourcing and procurement program delivers. It does so via providing both direct cost savings as well as corresponding KPIs. In addition, the story provides detailed data that allows you to identify root causes for process gaps, allowing you to drive continuous improvement.

The story consists of 5 pages, covering 

  • Value Analytics customer gain visibility into the ROI they've achieved using Ariba
  • Cost Savings shows spend that has been strategically sourced, price reduction from sourcing events and guided buying RFQs, savings rates as a % of sourced spend and trued-up savings.
  • Spend Compliance tracks year-over-year improvement to compliant spend put through proper buy channels
  • Working Capital measure captured and lost discounts across supply base
  • Process Improvement measure cycle times for all critical source to pay subprocesses like supplier onboarding, sourcing project, contract execution, REQ to PO and invoice processing

Savings/Benefits Analysis Summary

Strategic Spend Overview - Process Improvement

2. SAP Ariba Source to Pay - Procurement Operational Spend Overview

This story uncovers the untapped potential of your procurement practice, gaining valuable insights through a holistic operational view of your source to pay process. It will help you to maximize your end-to-end procurement efficiency, mitigate supplier risks and optimize category spend.

The story consists of 5 pages, covering 

  • Supplier Management: Onboarding of suppliers, risk exposure, geographical distribution, registration and qualification status
  • Sourcing: Awarded amounts per event/commodity and risk attached, event leading and pending savings, event participation over time and events per event owner
  • Contracts: Contract amounts per project owner/commodity and attached supplier risks, cycle times per project, distribution of contract types and contract expiration term types, overview of expiring contracts
  • Buying: spend details per cost center/commodity over time, contract vs. non-contract spend, catalog vs. non-catalog spend, approval cycle times , rejections rates and approval time per approver
  • Invoicing: Inovoice amounts per cost center/commodity/over time and cash flow related, amounts per due date

Operational Spend Overview - Supplier Management

Operational Spend Overview - Contracts


3. SAP Ariba Source to Pay - Supplier 360

This story allows you to gain a complete 360 view over your suppliers. ​
Discover a panoramic perspective of your suppliers related procurement spend, from a risk, a geography or even an individual supplier angle. This will help you to identify top-performing suppliers, mitigate supplier risks and increase your suppliers’ overall process compliance.

Supplier 360 Story

 

SAP Datasphere Models

Once imported you can find all related objects in the SAP_CONTENT space. 

The stories in SAP Analytics Cloud and their data models in SAP Datasphere combine data from various Ariba Solutions like:

Supplier Management

  • SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance
  • SAP Ariba Supplier Risk

Sourcing and Contracts

  • SAP Ariba Sourcing
  • SAP Ariba Contracts

Procurement

  • SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing
  • SAP Ariba Guided Buying (Tactical Sourcing)

Please check the image below to get a first understanding of what is included. 

Overview SAP Datasphere Models

The objects have roughly been separated to reflect applications in the SAP Ariba suite, using the following naming convention:

SM -> Supplier Management

SC -> Sourcing & Contracts

BI -> Buying & Invoicing 

Here is one example for analyzing your Invoice line items (based on Analytical API data). It will help you to sort your invoices according to self defined aging buckets and to identify invoices with exceptions easily. 

Data modeling has followed the layered approach as for general content development in SAP Datasphere:

In the Inbound Layer (IL) you will find the local table SAP Ariba: Invoice Line Items (LT) (SAP_ARIBA_SRC_INVOICELINEITEMS_AN) and an abstracting view on top SAP Ariba: Invoice Line Items - A (IL) (SAP_PROC_BI_IL_InvoiceLineItems) that adds realm specifica like the reporting currency.

In the Harmonization Layer (HL) on top, you will find the Fact View that combines the Invoice Line Item data with the invoice exceptions plus the Date Grid view that "attaches" the dynamic date buckets to your invoice data to let you check on due dates for your invoices.

In the Reporting Layer (RL) on top, the Analytic Model SAP Ariba: Invoice Line Items - A (RL) (SAP_PROC_BI_RL_InvoiceLineItems) defines additional calculated measures and exposes the data to your SAP Analytics Cloud story.

Invoice Line Items with due date aging information

Aging Invoices Per Supplier

In case you are operating on multiple realms, don't shy away, this has been catered for throughout the data models. So you can report on single or multiple realms.

 

Data Integration

How to get your SAP Ariba data into SAP Datasphere?

The data is sourced from SAP Ariba using the following APIs:

  • Analytical API
  • Operational Reporting API for Procurement
  • Operational Reporting API for Strategic Sourcing
  • Supplier Data API
  • Risk Exposure API
  • Master Data Retrieval API for Procurement

For replicating the data from your SAP Ariba applications the Ariba Extractor app is used. The Ariba Extractor is a BTP application  that you can deploy on your BTP account. It can be modified and extended by customers and partners as they see fit.

Please see the image below to get a better idea of the set up.

Data Integration from SAP Ariba to SAP Datasphere

Once set up, you can schedule load jobs to have the data replicated into tables of an HDI container that is attached to your SAP Datasphere tenant. The SAP Datasphere models are based on top of these tables.

To get started, you can check out this detailed discovery mission that explains the set up and replication process in detail.

 

Content Documentation

Now where can you get more info on the analytics package?

For installing the packages on SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere side and getting started please check out the documentation. This will also give you an overview of all data models included.

You can find more details on the Value Analytics for SAP Ariba Procurement content package in

SAP Analytics Cloud Content Documentation

SAP Datasphere Business Content Documentation

 

Hope you enjoyed reading this blog describing the new Value Analytics for SAP Ariba Procurement package. For feedback and enhancement ideas for the package, please reach out to us at  sap_analytics_cloud_content@sap.com

Also if you have questions and want to share with other users feel free to post a question in the community:

  • For SAP Analytics Cloud, please start here.
  • For SAP Datasphere, please start here

entry.title: SAP Ariba Procure to Order 2408 Release Key Innovations
entry.content:
Welcome to our 2408 Innovation blog, we are thrilled to share with you some exciting new features and improvements coming your way in the Procure to Order space. Let's dive into some of the key highlights that we hope will bring even more value to your procurement processes

SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App - Purchase Requisition Approvals now available

With the role-based experience in the new SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app, approvers now have the convenience of easily finding requisitions in their to-do list, viewing line-item details, and then approving or rejecting them, all from within the mobile app. This eliminates the need to switch to the SAP Ariba web app for this task, offering a more streamlined and user-friendly experience.

  If you are eager for more information, you can browse this blog post on how to get ready for mobile shopping!

 

SAP Ariba Buying, guided buying capability - Copy Additional RFQ Information to Requisitions

This enhancement means that Guided buying can now copy more information from RFQ events to purchase requisitions, so users and suppliers have all the necessary information for ordered items or services.

This means that critical fields on guided sourcing event line items like RFQ ID, Comments, Start and End Date, and Attachments are now seamlessly transferred from RFQ to Requisition, providing enhanced visibility and compliance for both buyers and suppliers through the source to order process.

 

SAP Ariba Buying, guided buying capability - Support for OData V4 APIs in the 2NV and 3EN Integration Scenarios

This is a vital feature and enabler for our S/4HANA or SAP S/4 HANA Cloud integration customers, as It provides an adoption path for the current state requisition integrations as the version 2 oData API has been deprecated.

This feature is an enabler for the other integration related features we cover in this blog for Guided Buying Capability with SAP Ariba Buying (2NV) and Guided Buying for Central Procurement with SAP Ariba Buying (‏3EN‏).

For more technical information please see oData version 4 details, version comparison

 

SAP Ariba Buying, guided buying capability - Ability to classify Attachments as Internal or External Documents in Guided Buying with SAP S/4HANA

In the Guided Buying Capability with SAP Ariba Buying integration scenarios Buyers can now indicate whether line-item level attachments are made visible to suppliers.

This critical indicator is sent to S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud to ensure that only those attachments flagged as external are shared with suppliers when a purchase order is distributed to the SAP Business Network. This enhances the experience for buyers and suppliers, bridging the current gap and providing enhanced control and clarity over document sharing.

 

SAP Ariba Buying, guided buying capability - Improved Shipping Information for Guided Buying Requisitions with SAP S/4HANA

Previously, only basic shipping information was sent to SAP S/4HANA in the 2NV and 3EN integration scenarios where a delivery address was not defined as a plant, leaving potential gaps in key delivery information.

With this improvement, additional shipping details such as phone numbers, fax numbers, and email addresses are now included, facilitating more comprehensive and detailed communication capabilities between business entities ultimately helping users receive items or services more quickly.

This capability is enabled by the oData version 4 API previously mentioned, where additional fields in delivery information can now be leveraged. More supporting information for this integration scenario can be found here

 

We hope you are excited about these impactful new features and enhancements in SAP Ariba 2408 release. We encourage you to explore and adopt where relevant to your Ariba landscape, to further optimize your procurement processes and drive more value for your organization.

Please check out the other blogs, and subscribe to the authors for latest updates as well as the following resources on our YouTube channels:

  • SAP Products and Services YouTube channel - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH
  • "Spend Management and Business Network" playlist - https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH&si=zTlYobcledkWNQ2M

entry.title: SAP Ariba 2408 Key Innovations Preview
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba community!

We’re thrilled to introduce several innovative features designed to enhance your procurement processes. These updates focus on improving visibility, efficiency, and integration across our SAP Ariba solutions. For those of you looking for a deeper dive into specific functionalities, we’ll also provide links to detailed blogs that explore a more comprehensive view for specific areas. Let's dive right in!

Master Data Overview in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management (CIM-1428)

With the new Display Imported Master Data app, you can now view all master data along with replication details imported to SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management from connected systems. This feature addresses the need for visibility over replicated master data, ensuring that you can validate and monitor data replication efficiently.

Extract Multiple PO References at Header Level (CIM-431)

Invoice processing just got a lot smoother. The Extract at Header Level Multiple PO References from Invoice feature utilizes SAP AI Business Services' Document Information Extraction to pull multiple PO reference values directly at the header level of an invoice. This advancement enhances automation and accuracy in handling invoices, helping you save time and reduce manual workload.

Duplicate Invoice Detection (CIM-220)

Enhance the reliability of your invoice processing automation with the Duplicate Invoice Detection feature. This function checks fields such as company code, invoicing party, external reference, and invoice date in draft invoices against existing invoices during editing or transfer. It automatically flags duplicates, preventing errors and potential fraud.

Purchase Requisition Approvals in SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App (SAS-318)

Efficiency on the go! With the Support for Purchase Requisition Approvals in the SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app, approvers can easily search for requisitions in their to-do list, view line-item details, and approve or reject them through a role-based user interface. This feature ensures that crucial approval workflows are accessible anytime, anywhere. Read more about how this feature can help streamline your mobile procurement processes in SAP Ariba Procure to Order 2408 Release Key Innovations

SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App The - Purchase Requisition Approvals screen

 

Supplier Risk Segmentation and Analysis (ARI-20913)

Gain critical insights into your suppliers' risks with the Supplier Risk Segmentation and Analysis feature. This tool allows you to view and analyze suppliers based on country/region and commodity risk, and easily export segmentation reports to a spreadsheet.

The Segmented View page with the new Create Engagement Request button

 

Import Country Risk Score to Configure Risk Exposure (ARI-23844)

Customize your risk assessment with the Import Country Risk Score to Configure Risk Exposure feature. You now have the flexibility to use your own source of country risk score information, rather than solely relying on default data. Tailor your risk exposure configurations to better align with your organization's specific criteria and needs. For more information on SAP Ariba Supplier Management features, take a look at the SAP Ariba Supplier Management 2408 Release Key Innovations Preview

Viewing Supplier Risk Level in SAP Ariba Category Management (CAT-5800)

Understanding supplier risk levels is vital for strategic category management. The new integration between SAP Ariba Category Management and SAP Ariba Supplier Risk allows category managers to view and assess supplier risk levels (Low, Medium, High). This visibility is essential for making informed decisions and strategizing effectively.

The new Risk Level available in the Category Suppliers page

Integrate Cost Structure Data from External Market Intelligence Providers (CAT-7893)

Enhance your cost structure analysis with the ability to integrate Cost Structure Data from External Market Intelligence Providers. Category management administrators can now incorporate external cost structure data directly into the Cost Structure tool within the strategy and plan document. This addition allows for more comprehensive and accurate strategy formulation. To delve deeper into this and other SAP Ariba Category Management, SAP Ariba guided sourcing, and SAP Ariba Contract Management, check out our SAP Ariba Source to Contract 2408 Release Key Innovations Preview

With these features, we are aiming to streamline your procurement processes, enhance risk management, and provide deeper insights into supplier relationships. We encourage you to explore these exciting updates and implement them into your workflows to harness their full potential. Stay tuned for more updates by subscribing to myself and our other fantastic bloggers and take a look at the links below.

  • Register for the Early Release Series for 2408 release
  • Resources:
    • Feature related materials
      • SAP Ariba Connect - Release Readiness (Customers except CIM)
      • Readiness Community page (For CIM customers)
    • SAP Community for Intelligent Spend Management page
    • SAP Help Portal pages - Procurement and Network
    • What’s New
  • Encourage customers to check out the other blogs, and subscribe to the authors for latest updates.
  • Introduce customers to
    • SAP Products and Services YouTube channel - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH
    • "Spend Management and Business Network" playlist - https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH&si=zTlYobcledkWNQ2M

entry.content:

entry.title: SAP Ariba Shopping App or SAP Ariba Procurement App? What fits best for me?
entry.content:
Hello Ariba Community,

This blog post aims to offer a comprehensive comparison of the functionality and product support provided by the SAP Ariba Shopping app and the SAP Ariba Procurement app, assisting you in determining which app is best suited for your specific procurement needs.

In essence, the SAP Ariba Shopping app, developed on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), is a mobile native application that empowers employees and managers to navigate and shop from company catalogs. It is worth noting that the forthcoming 2411 update will introduce support for Level 1 punchout catalogs.

The shopping experience is comparable with shopping apps for consumers, therefore only minimal training is required to use the app. As shopping should be simple and fun you have certain restrictions on catalog item types and the ability of changing the purchase requisition. For those use cases, we recommend using the SAP Ariba Procurement app which is designed for procurement professionals and employees with advanced shopping needs such as ordering configurable items or doing an account split on item level.

Good news is that you can use both apps in parallel either for one user or for different user groups.

Let's first delve into the commercial conditions. The table below illustrates the Ariba products supported by each app:

 

Product                             

SAP Ariba Shopping

SAP Ariba Procurement

SAP Ariba Buying

supported

supported

SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing

supported

supported

SAP Ariba SNAP

 

supported

SAP Ariba Sourcing

 

supported

SAP Ariba Contracts

 

supported

SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance Management

 

supported

If your organization has subscribed any of the listed products, you are entitled to use the app without incurring any additional license costs. However, it is important to be aware of any potential limitations in how the apps are utilizing the supported Ariba products:

 

Product / Product Scope

SAP Ariba Shopping

SAP Ariba Procurement

SAP Ariba Buying

Support for company catalogs

Support for company catalogs

SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing

Support for company catalogs

Support for company catalogs

SAP Ariba SNAP

 

Support for company catalogs

SAP Ariba Buying with guided buying capability

Support for company catalogs and planned for 2411 punchout L1

Not supported:

  • Intelligent product recommendations
  • Policies
  • Favorites
  • Guided Buying tiles

Support for company catalogs.

Not supported:

  • Intelligent product recommendations
  • Policies
  • Favorites
  • Guided Buying tiles

Integration of guided buying with S/4 HANA or S/4 HANA Cloud (2NV/3EN scenario)

Both apps do not support requisitions when guided buying is integrated with SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud (2NV/3EN)

Both apps do not support requisitions created when guided buying is integrated with SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud  (2NV/3EN)

SAP Ariba Sourcing

 

Task management is supported

SAP Ariba Contracts

 

Task management is supported

SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance Management

  • New supplier creation
  • Supplier request tracking

 

Here is the functionality comparison of shopping for both apps:

 

Shopping Functionality

SAP Ariba Procurement

Buy again

 

supported

Add account distributions

 

supported

Shop on behalf of another user

 

supported

Post Goods Receipt

 

supported

Change Purchase order

 

supported

 

Lastly, let's explore the roadmap for both applications. We are currently revising our roadmaps but one direction we can share is that we are thriving to enable Joule integration into our mobile app portfolio. As soon as we have clarity, I will share the roadmap items in a new blog post.

Enjoy shopping!

 

Michael Spiess

SAP Ariba Product Management

 

Further References:

  • New blog post about the latest release V1.12.0

entry.title: Integration of custom fields in standard Master Data Object using ARBCIG_MASTER_DATA_EXPORT program
entry.content:
Integration of custom fields in standard Master Data Object using ARBCIG_MASTER_DATA_EXPORT program

 

Ariba Procurement or Buying and Invoicing is often integrated with backend SAP ERP system. In such cases source of truth for master data is SAP ERP. CIG Addon provided by SAP has a master data export program named “ARBCIG_MASTER_DATA_EXPORT” which fetch the data from SAP convert it into a required format resulting in generation of csv files and then sends it to Ariba through CIG to upload the data in Ariba.

The master data export program is designed to extract the standard fields required in Ariba, any custom field added in Ariba is not supported using out of the box integration, however customization can be added in SAP ERP to send these custom fields from SAP ERP to Ariba if it exists in SAP ERP, which helps in reducing manual efforts of uploading the updated files in Ariba.

To understand how its done, let us consider an example where "Profit Center" and "Cost Center Category" fields are passed as custom fields in standard extract of cost center.

The first step is identifying the function module and structure that master data export program uses to export cost center information.

Here FM is “ARBCIG_COST_CENTER_EXPORT” and structure is “ARBCIG_COST_CENTER”. The structure has 6 fields as shown below.

Standard Structure "ARBCIG_COST_CENTER"

 

Now there are two ways, enhancement can be added in SAP ERP. Either utilize any existing fields to pass profit center and cost center category or create a custom field in the structure to pass these two fields.

Here we decided to add a custom field “ZPRCTR” with data element as “PRCTR” and “ZKOSAR” with data element as “KOSAR” to capture profit center and cost center category mapped to each cost center. Reason for creating a custom field is inability to use the existing fields.

Once the custom fields are added, structure looks as below.

Structure "ARBCIG_COST_CENTER" with Custom Fields

 

Once the structure is ready, next step is to write the logic to fill the data in “ZPRCTR” and “ZKOSAR” fields.

To do this “ARBCIG_MASTERDATA” BAdI can be used. Here we need to use method “IF_EX_ARBCIG_MASTERDATA~PUBLISH_COST_CENTER” to write the logic. Here logic to be written such a way that it updates the data in COST_CENTER_INFO and COST_CENTER_DEL elements so that data gets updated properly for incremental scenario as well.

Now execute the master data export program in test mode and observe the generated file. The file in AL11 looks as shown below.

Generated CostCenter.csv File

 

Here we can notice, additional columns ZPRCTR and ZKOSAR have been added in the file. Now it is important to validate the column name configured in Ariba. To do so, first ensure that, custom fields are already added in Ariba and then download the data dictionary to get the file column name of field profit center and cost center category in CostCenter.csv file.

Details of CostCenter.csv as per Data Dictionary

 

The highlighted rows in above image indicate complete details about custom fields, whereas highlighted cells indicate column name for the field Profit Center and Cost Center Category in CostCenter.csv file. Since here the column names are different than the structure’s field name it is necessary to add the data in “ARBCIG_FIELDMAP” table to replace ZPRCTR with "cus_ProftCenter" and ZKOSAR with "cus_CostCenterCategory".

 

Add below information in ARBCIG_FIELDMAP table,

Solution: AR Ariba Procurement

Structure: ARBCIG_COST_CENTER

Field SAP: ZPRCTR

Field Ariba: cus_ProfitCenter

 

Solution: AR Ariba Procurement

Structure: ARBCIG_COST_CENTER

Field SAP: ZKOSAR

Field Ariba: cus_CostCenterCategory

 

Once done ARBCIG_FIELDMAP table looks as shown below.

Updated Entries in "ARBCIG_FIELDMAP" Table

 

Now execute master data export once again and validate the file once again, notice column name has been updated as specified in ARBCIG_FIELDMAP table.

Generated CostCenter.csv with Updated Column Header

 

If no errors found execute the program without test mode. Download the cost center extract from Ariba to validate the custom data is successfully uploaded.

CostCenterExport.csv

 

ARBCIG_MASTERDATA BAdI and ARBCIG_FIELDMAP table are the two important tools SAP has provided to perform any customization to master data which is not available OOTB. Using this technique multiple customizations can be achieved as per business requirement.

entry.title: Discover SAP Business Technology Platform at Spend Connect Live
entry.content:
Spend Connect Live and Spend Connect Virtual are quickly approaching, and I can hardly contain my excitement! There you will be able to see the application of SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) through expert sessions and customers stories. To prepare, here’s a summary of what SAP Business Technology Platform will be showing.

In the world of procurement, businesses need to find ways to make smarter spend decisions. According to a recent IDC research study sponsored by SAP, Overall, companies saw a 495% three-year return, taking only an average of six months to pay back on their investment by improving cross-organizational efficiencies, creating value through use of data, and more using SAP BTP.

Discover how SAP BTP can impact your Spend systems

If you’re new to SAP Business Technology Platform, Spend Connect Live has several sessions and resources that can help you discover SAP BTP. Our BTP101: Intro to SAP Business Technology Platform session can help you understand how SAP BTP optimizes your procurement processes. You can futher expand on the applications of SAP BTP and view use cases relating to procurement, business network, and supply chain in the BTP201: Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP session. For those interested in Concur, Streamline T&E management with integration of SAP and SAP Concur solutions will highlight how automation and integration between your Concur and ERP solutions can be done using SAP BTP.

Still have questions? You can visit us at the SAP Business Technology Platform Demo Pod on the show floor for additional demos and time with experts.

Hear directly from SAP customers how they benefit from SAP BTP

Now that you know what SAP BTP can do with spend solutions, you’re most likely now wondering how you can apply it to your business. Here are some notable customer use cases you can read, as well as some of the customer success stories being presented at Spend Connect Live.

Customer use case: Infosys, a leading provider improving supplier management

Infosys, an SAP customer, partner, and a global leader in technology consulting, has also improved supplier management. By using SAP BTP, Infosys were able to develop four custom applications for suppliers that allowed the integration of their many SAP solutions. With over 3,000 of their suppliers onboarded, they were able to decrease the onboarding time by 50%—all with one interface. Suppliers are now able to effectively use SAP Business Network for their needs, and Infosys can improve their supplier relations and processes.

Customer use case: Blue Diamond Growers, the biggest almond processor in the world optimizing key processes

Blue Diamond Growers  is the largest almond processor and marketer in the world, and are utilizing SAP BTP to support their over 3,000 suppliers and 1,800 employees by providing real-time data using SAP Analytics Cloud and automating manual processes using SAP Build Process Automation. Together with SAP Integration Suite, they are able to optimize key processes across procurement, inventory management, and quality control.

Spend Connect Live session: How Richemont enhanced supplier collaboration

At Spend Connect Live there are customer sessions highlighting how SAP BTP has elevated their procurement processes. At the session Growing supplier collaboration at Richemont to gain supply chain visibility, you can see firsthand how luxury goods group Richemont has contributed their improved performance to enhanced supplier collaboration using SAP BTP.

Spend Connect Live session: How Endress+Hauser digitized their asset lifestyle

Another customer session to note is Transforming asset collaboration at Endress+Hauser. At this session you can learn how the global leader of automations and measurement technology has digitized their asset lifecycle using SAP BTP, and they will be showcasing the benefits of using a platform along with SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration.  

Spend Connect Live session: Sony improves their cost management

Finally, technology conglomerate Sony will be presenting in Optimizing efficiency at Sony with SAP Ariba solutions and SAP BTP, where they will showcase how they utilized source-to-pay SAP Ariba solutions and SAP BTP to improve their cost management.

Learn more about SAP BTP for Spend and more virtually at Spend Connect Virtual

With SAP BTP being a unified cloud-based platform, it can optimize the processes for both companies and their suppliers, whether it be by improving visibility into procurement operations or integrating solutions for a seamless experience. As your business grows, you will need a platform that grows with you—Be sure to attend or watch the BTP101 and BTP201 sessions for the foundational knowledge of SAP BTP for spend. In anticipation for Spend Connect Live and Spend Connect Virtual you can learn more about SAP BTP for Spend at sap.com/btp-for-spend.

I’m looking forward to seeing you all at Spend Connect Live! Can’t make it in person? Register for Spend Connect Virtual here.

entry.title: Released now: Approve employees purchases with IF Design Award winning SAP Ariba Shopping App!
entry.content:
Dear Ariba Community,  

We are pleased to share the exciting news that employee shopping has become even more efficient with the launch of SAP Ariba Shopping version 1.12.0. In addition to this milestone, we are honored to announce that we have received the IF Design Award 2024 for our innovative and seamless buyer shopping experience!

With the latest release, users can now leverage the entire purchasing process through their mobile devices, from initiating the purchase request to receiving the goods at their designated location, using SAP Ariba Buying or SAP Ariba Buying, with guided buying capabilities. 

Let's take a closer look at how this workflow benefits an employee – let us take Colby from the latest YouTube video as an example - looking to order office equipment, such as a new phone. Whether utilizing SAP Ariba Buying or SAP Ariba Buying, with guided buying capabilities, Colby can easily start shopping by browsing through a selection of company catalogs within the app. Furthermore, company catalogs provide valuable supplementary information, including details on sustainability or even an augmented reality view of the items, if provided by the catalog provider. Additionally, with the upcoming Ariba release 2411, Colby will have access to Level 1 punchout catalogs, significantly expanding the range of available products for mobile shopping.

Once Colby has added the chosen items to his shopping cart and reviewed his selection, he can submit a purchase requisition to initiate the approval process within Ariba Buying. Based on the configured approval workflow, Colby's manager will receive an approval notification. This notification can be previewed on the manager's phone, and can be easily accessed with a single tab to proceed to the approval screen within the SAP Ariba Shopping app. From there, the manager can approve the purchase requisition, signaling the green light for the delivery of the requested items to the employee.  

We encourage you to explore the approval workflow within the demo mode of the SAP Ariba Shopping app. Should you wish to test the approval functionality with your Ariba system, please read this blog post or the onboarding guide at the SAP help documentation to get started with SAP Ariba Shopping. You find more details how to setup Ariba user mobile approver roles at this help page.

Please note that the approval functionality is still available within the SAP Ariba Procurement app. For a comprehensive comparison of the two apps, we recommend reading our new blog post.

Enjoy shopping!

 

Michael Spiess

SAP Ariba Product Management 

 

Further References:

  • New blog post about app comparison between SAP Ariba Shopping and SAP Ariba Procurement app
  • Download (iOS/Android) SAP Ariba Shopping app 
  • Watch SAP Ariba Shopping App on Youtube
  • Our roadmaps for iOS and Android.
  • Blog post: Gettings started with SAP Ariba Shopping App in 7 steps

entry.title: 2411 Early Release Series for SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network releases
entry.content:
Sign up now for the 2411 Early Release Series

SAP strives to equip our customers and partners with valuable release readiness resources for each SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network quarterly release.

Follow this blog to receive notification of new series as soon as registration opens.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Early Release Series
The SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network Early Release series of webcasts cover features planned for the upcoming quarterly release.  This platform, available to customers and partners, has a clear mission: deliver an interactive forum to engage with SAP product experts providing simple, direct answers to your most pressing questions.  There is a different session for each product area with content tailored for the specific audience:

  • Supplier Management & Risk
  • Procure-to-Pay
  • Source-to-Contract
  • Business Network

If you want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts, we encourage you to participate in these informative sessions.  Register now!

Quarterly Release Communications
Notice of upcoming quarterly releases will be communicated to Buyer customers three weeks prior to the release date.  Suppliers receive notice two weeks prior.  Releases are communicated via email notification (look for subject line starting with: Advance Release Notice).

Designated Support Contacts (DSC) and anyone from your organization that has chosen to subscribe to product notifications will receive these announcements. 

The release calendar for SAP Business Network  and release calendar SAP Ariba Applications identify release timing several quarters in advance.

Where to find out more details?
On demand replays of webcasts from completed quarterly releases can be consumed from Release Readiness Resources: Webcast Archive Page

entry.title: SAP Ariba 2411 Release Key Innovations Preview
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba community!

We're thrilled to announce some fantastic new features and enhancements for Q4 2024 across various SAP Ariba modules. These updates are designed to streamline your procurement processes, enhance supplier management, and provide deeper insights into supplier relationships. Let's take a high-level look at the most impactful innovations in this release.

For those of you wanting to explore specific functionalities in detail, we have provided links to dedicated blogs that dive deeper into each area. Let's take a look!

SAP Spend Control Tower

We're excited to share the latest development with you SAP Spend Control Tower. This new tool is designed to merge all your payment and supplier data into a single source of truth, enriched with AI-enabled spend classification and supplier insights. With intuitive dashboards and advanced machine learning models, it’s easier than ever to streamline our processes, cut procurement costs, manage supplier risks, and keep an eye on our diversity and sustainability goals.

Having a unified data source might just be the key to overcoming some of our biggest challenges. SAP Spend Control Tower takes the powerful analytics and reporting capabilities of the old SAP Ariba Spend Analysis solution and pushes them even further. If you're curious to learn more about how it could help us transform our spend management, I'd recommend checking out the full details on our SAP Spend Control Tower product page.

Spend overview dashboard in SAP Spend Control Tower

SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance and Supplier Risk

Intelligent Recommendations Based on Supplier Risk Score

SAP Ariba Supplier Risk now offers intelligent recommendations for suppliers experiencing significant changes in risk scores. These new insights help buyers proactively identify and mitigate potential risks.

New recommendations section in the SAP Ariba Supplier Risk dashboard

SAP Business Network Certificate Information in the Supplier Profile Summary

The supplier profile summary now features added visibility into certificates maintained in SAP Business Network profiles. The new “Business Network Certificates” panel provides additional data points for supplier assessments, offering a comprehensive view directly from their SAP Business Network profile.

Business network certificates in the supplier profile summary

See the SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance & Supplier Risk 2411 Release: Key Feature Highlights blog for more information on these and other supplier management features

SAP Ariba Source to Contract

Enhancements to Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in SAP Ariba Category Management

We're excited to introduce enhancements to our Generative AI capabilities in SAP Ariba Category Management. You can now selectively generate articles for tools like Category Segmentation, Market Dynamics, and Cost Structure on demand, improving performance and reducing costs associated with producing AI-generated content.

Enhancements to Generative Artificial Intelligence in SAP Ariba Category Management

Ability to Create Category Strategy Details from the Develop Strategy and Plan App in SAP Ariba Category Management

Our new Category Strategy Details tool in the Develop Strategy and Plan app empowers category managers to create and manage multiple category-specific strategy details aligned with the overall business strategy.

Category strategy details in SAP Ariba Category Management

Ability to View, Analyze, and Manage Initiatives Across Categories in SAP Ariba Category Management

Manage your initiatives through the new Initiatives Overview app, providing comprehensive tools for tracking, optimizing, and visualizing data.

Initiatives overview in SAP Ariba Category Management

 

Create Optimization Scenarios for Multi-Round Bidding Events in Guided Sourcing

Generate optimization scenarios for multi-round bidding events, including editing constraints and updating scenarios with the latest data.

Enhancements to Grading and Scoring in Guided Sourcing

Improved grading features for guided sourcing, including offline grading for primary bids and rolled-up section level grading for alternate bids.

See the SAP Ariba Source to Contract 2411 Release Key Innovations Preview blog for more information on these and other source to contract features

SAP Ariba Procure to Order

Support for PunchOut Items in SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App

You can now purchase items from external supplier catalogs through Level 1 PunchOut catalogs directly from the SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app. This enhancement broadens your product range for mobile shopping while ensuring compliance and approvals are managed through the normal Ariba checkout process.

View and Edit Requisition Line Items in SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App

The SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app now provides detailed line item information in the requisition details screen and allows users to edit line items directly from the checkout screen. This update enhances flexibility and transparency in the mobile requisitioning process.

See the SAP Ariba Procure to Order 2411 Release Key Innovations blog for more information on these and other features

For further reading and resources, check out the links below and dive deeper into each specific innovation.

Resources:
    • Register for the Early Release Series for 2411 release
    • Resources:

        • SAP Ariba Connect - Release Readiness (Customers except CIM)
        • Readiness Community page (For CIM customers)
      • SAP Community for Intelligent Spend Management page
      • SAP Help Portal pages - Procurement and Network 
      • What’s New
